channel.title: Cariboo Signals
channel.copyright: © 2026 Erich Zirnhelt. Licensed under CC BY-NC 4.0.
channel.itunes:subtitle: Technology and society in rural BC with Riley and Casey
channel.itunes:author: Riley and Casey
channel.itunes:summary: How do rural communities grow alongside technology? Daily conversations about responsible tech progress in the Cariboo region on the traditional territories of the Secwépemc, Tŝilhqot'in, and Dakelh nations. Riley brings tech systems thinking with rural roots, while Casey focuses on community development. New episodes every day with weekly themes.
channel.description: How do rural communities grow alongside technology? Daily conversations about responsible tech progress in the Cariboo region on the traditional territories of the Secwépemc, Tŝilhqot'in, and Dakelh nations.

item.title: Indigenous Lands And Innovation
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Indigenous Lands & Innovation.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Williams Lake Tribune] B.C. First Nation at odds over ownership of historic Greater Victoria site and [Williams Lake Tribune] Barkerville 2026 season brings gold rush history to life with new attractions and events, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: When Indigenous communities share ecological knowledge with Western institutions, how can that sharing preserve Indigenous data sovereignty and prevent knowledge systems from being absorbed or devalued? Topics include: Data sovereignty and decolonizing data systems, Knowledge braiding versus integration, Indigenous fire stewardship and clam garden restoration, Traditional ecological knowledge versus Western scientific monitoring, Food sovereignty and ecosystem restoration projects, Funding gaps in Guardian Stewards and community-controlled programs.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: Denisiqi Services Society

Sources discussed:

  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] B.C. First Nation at odds over owner...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Barkerville 2026 season brings gold ...
  • InsideEVs: [InsideEVs] 'Megawatt' EV Chargers Have Landed In The U.S., ...
  • NPR Health News: [NPR Health News] A measles outbreak that began in rural Uta...
  • ScienceAlert: [ScienceAlert] Alien Megastructures May Be Physically Feasib...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] B.C. set to mark 10-year anniversary...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Out-of-control wildfire 50 hectares ...
  • Newsletter: Animikii Indigenous Technology : Braiding knowledge: how Indigenous expertise and western sci...

Additional sources provided:

  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Okanagan Humane Society: Happy Harle...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Princeton Posse punch ticket to KIJH...
  • Toms Guide: [Toms Guide] I use the ‘cheese prompt’ to instantly improve ...
  • NYT Business: [NYT Business] Half of Gen Z Uses AI, but Their Feelings Are...
  • Android Authority: [Android Authority] Gemini meets NotebookLM is Google’s late...
  • XDA Developers: [XDA Developers] This Raspberry Pi codes, clocks out, and ch...
  • ZDNet: [ZDNet] Why I stopped using 'Modern Standby' on my Windows l...
  • ScienceAlert: [ScienceAlert] New Simulations Reveal How Earth's Strongest ...
  • ZDNet: [ZDNet] KDE Linux is the purest form of Plasma I've tested
  • XDA Developers: [XDA Developers] The Pi Zero 2 W is the only Raspberry Pi th...
  • TechRadar: [TechRadar] 'It doesn’t catch fire': Why China’s "fireproof"...
  • ScienceAlert: [ScienceAlert] One Type of Dream Could Make Your Sleep Feel ...
  • Newsletter: Animikii Indigenous Technology : From wildfires to clam gardens: decolonizing data
  • Newsletter: Animikii Indigenous Technology : From wildfires to clam gardens: decolonizing data

Credits
Theme Song: Cariboo Signals by Suno
Content Curation & Script: Claude (Anthropic)
Script Review Model: claude-sonnet-4-6
TTS Voices: OpenAI TTS API
Cover Art: ChatGPT (OpenAI)
Podcast Coordination: Erich Zirnhelt
© 2026 Erich Zirnhelt. Licensed under CC BY-NC 4.0.

item.title: Community Tech And Governance
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Community Tech & Governance.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Williams Lake Tribune] Annual cleaning of water mains and reservoirs begins in Williams Lake and [Quesnel Cariboo Observer] UPDATE: 3 arts positions reinstated at Okanagan College following arbitration, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: What governance failures are revealed when Indigenous communities must resort to litigation to protect their territories from industrial contamination, and what infrastructure would enable proactive rather than reactive protection? Topics include: Monitoring authority and data control as governance levers, Litigation as a governance mechanism when regulatory systems fail, Funding sustainability for nation-led environmental infrastructure, The gap between legal rights frameworks and operational capacity to exercise them, Treaty negotiation versus alternative pathways to jurisdiction.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: Community Futures Cariboo Chilcotin

Sources discussed:

  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Annual cleaning of water mains and r...
  • Quesnel Cariboo Observer: [Quesnel Cariboo Observer] UPDATE: 3 arts positions reinstat...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] George Abbott named chief commission...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Williams Lake invited to eat around ...
  • XDA Developers: [XDA Developers] The ESP32 Flash Bee is a palm-sized lightni...
  • Gizmodo: [Gizmodo] Tiny Robots Reveal Hidden Ocean Chemistry in Low-O...
  • New Atlas: [New Atlas] New cold-hardy electrolyte could potentially dou...
  • IndigiNews: [IndigiNews] Film uplifts ceremonies for the return of oolig...
  • ScienceAlert: [ScienceAlert] New Breakthrough in Solar Cell Efficiency Hit...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] B.C.-based mining company confirms 9...
  • The Tyee: [The Tyee] A Piikani Member Vows to Sue to Stop Selenium Pol...

Additional sources provided:

  • XDA Developers: [XDA Developers] This tiny Docker container monitors my enti...
  • Quartz: [Quartz] RKLB's Neutron Launch Vehicle: Is It Driving the Ne...
  • Nautilus: [Nautilus] The Mystery of Water on the Moon
  • TechRadar: [TechRadar] 'Stolen session cookies render MFA irrelevant': ...
  • InsideEVs: [InsideEVs] The Next BMW i7 Will Get Its Batteries From An U...
  • Gizmodo: [Gizmodo] Unprecedented Observation Reveals 2 Supermassive B...
  • Hackaday: [Hackaday] TinyGo Boldly Goes Where No Go Ever Did Go Before
  • Hackaday: [Hackaday] Magnetic Levitation using an Induction Cooktop
  • How-To Geek: [How-To Geek] Your NAS has USB ports: 4 practical uses most ...
  • ZDNet: [ZDNet] Love window snapping on Linux? You should try a tili...
  • ZDNet: [ZDNet] Why I returned to Enlightenment Linux after 30 years...
  • Hackaday: [Hackaday] Thermostat? Do It With a 555!

Credits

RURAL CONNECTIONS: What governance failures are revealed when Indigenous communities must resort to litigation to protect their territories from industrial contamination, and what infrastructure would enable proactive rather than reactive protection? Topics include: Monitoring authority and data control as governance levers, Litigation as a governance mechanism when regulatory systems fail, Funding sustainability for nation-led environmental infrastructure, The gap between legal rights frameworks and operational capacity to exercise them, Treaty negotiation versus alternative pathways to jurisdiction.

item.title: Working Lands And Industry
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Working Lands & Industry.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Williams Lake Tribune] Victoria seeks federal, provincial input to develop ‘reasonable guardrails’ for AI and [Williams Lake Tribune] Williams Lake businesses and homeowners encouraged to FireSmart, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: How can BC's regulatory framework be restructured to prevent harm from overlapping Crown land uses (forestry, ranching, mining) rather than simply compensating victims after the fact? Topics include: Cattle poisoning settlement as precedent and its limitations, Data sovereignty and documentation as leverage in resource disputes, Crown land fragmentation across provincial ministries and coordination failures, Indigenous co-governance and First Nations' potential conflict resolution role, Organized collective bargaining versus atomized individual permit holders.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: Cariboo Agricultural Research Alliance

Sources discussed:

  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Victoria seeks federal, provincial i...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Williams Lake businesses and homeown...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Williams Lake community shows its he...
  • ScienceAlert: [ScienceAlert] It's Official: Artemis II Has Carried Humans ...
  • How-To Geek: [How-To Geek] Rivian's R2 pulls off something Tesla hasn't: ...
  • CFJC Today Kamloops: [CFJC Today Kamloops] Cariboo-Chilcotin MLA Doerkson’s 100 M...
  • 100 Mile Free Press: [100 Mile Free Press] Green Lake Community Club’s Pancake Br...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] 10 hectare wildfire sparks near Lytt...
  • ScienceAlert: [ScienceAlert] Earth's Population Has Surpassed The Planet's...
  • The Tyee: [The Tyee] Cattle Were Poisoned by BC’s Forest Fertilizer. N...

Additional sources provided:

  • ScienceAlert: [ScienceAlert] One Sleep Habit Could Boost Your Heart Health...
  • CNET: [CNET] NASA's Artemis II Breaks Record With Trip Around The ...
  • XDA Developers: [XDA Developers] This fingernail-sized ESP32 dev board ignor...
  • TechRadar: [TechRadar] 'We want to raise awareness on this issue': Goog...
  • NYT Well: [NYT Well] Federal Agency Unveils Three Potential Osteoarthr...
  • XDA Developers: [XDA Developers] Chasing the perfect home lab is why yours n...
  • WIRED: [WIRED] A Single Strike Won’t Shut Off the Gulf’s Desalinati...
  • Toms Guide: [Toms Guide] Netflix’s new AI doesn’t create videos — it rew...
  • Nautilus: [Nautilus] Did This 17th-Century Novel Presage the Coming Ar...
  • How-To Geek: [How-To Geek] I'm a homelab enthusiast, but I refuse to self...
  • Gizmodo: [Gizmodo] NASA Administrator Says Odds of Finding Alien Life...
  • ScienceAlert: [ScienceAlert] Droughts Are The Ideal Breeding Ground For An...
  • Kottke.org: [Kottke.org] A visual history of exploring the far side of t...
  • New Atlas: [New Atlas] Toyota’s new flagship EV is off to a flying star...

Topics include: Cattle poisoning settlement as precedent and its limitations, Data sovereignty and documentation as leverage in resource disputes, Crown land fragmentation across provincial ministries and coordination failures, Indigenous co-governance and First Nations' potential conflict resolution role, Organized collective bargaining versus atomized individual permit holders.

item.title: Arts, Culture And Digital Storytelling
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Arts, Culture & Digital Storytelling.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Williams Lake Tribune] Adam Sandler spotted in B.C. while filming movie and [Williams Lake Tribune] Robert Barron column: I hope I get to see humans walk on Mars, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: How should rural and Indigenous communities build digital cultural infrastructure that serves their storytelling needs while avoiding both corporate platform gatekeeping and unsustainable technological dependence? Topics include: Ireland's government digital wallet age verification system and identity gatekeeping implications, Algorithmic attention economics as a form of cultural gatekeeping versus government-mandated identity systems, Data sovereignty and Indigenous control over cultural archival materials, Regional digital cultural infrastructure co-op model with shared hosting and technical capacity, Digital preservation sustainability and technology obsolescence risks for cultural projects.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: Central Cariboo Arts & Culture Society

Sources discussed:

  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Adam Sandler spotted in B.C. while f...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Robert Barron column: I hope I get t...
  • Quesnel Cariboo Observer: [Quesnel Cariboo Observer] A look back at historic Quesnel: ...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] ‘Asinine’: First Nations leaders rea...
  • 100 Mile Free Press: [100 Mile Free Press] 100 Mile House man proposes alternate ...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Canadian Armed Forces complete large...
  • Engadget: [Engadget] Ireland is testing out a digital wallet that cond...
  • Open Culture: [Open Culture] Watch the Titanic and Lusitania Sink in Real ...

Additional sources provided:

  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] QUIZ: A celebration of Easter
  • Business Insider: [Business Insider] I quit tech, bought 22 acres, and didn't ...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Langford diver goes deep in the Saan...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Land Act: Notice of Application for ...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] William Joseph Eli Dye (Willie)
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Outbreaks hit 6 care facilities in I...
  • fundsforNGOs: [fundsforNGOs] Curated List of Funding Opportunities in Agri...

Credits
Theme Song: Cariboo Signals by Suno
Content Curation & Script: Claude (Anthropic)
Script Review Model: claude-sonnet-4-6
TTS Voices: OpenAI TTS API
Cover Art: ChatGPT (OpenAI)
Podcast Coordination: Erich Zirnhelt
© 2026 Erich Zirnhelt. Licensed under CC BY-NC 4.0.

item.title: Resilient Rural Futures
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Resilient Rural Futures.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Hackaday] The Smallest Dialup ISP is a Raspberry Pi and a Prison Phone and [XDA Developers] A single mini PC replaced my Raspberry Pi cluster and nearly cut my power use in half, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: When precision agriculture technology works effectively, how should rural farmers engage with the data ownership and platform dependency structures that come with it? Topics include: Data ownership and control in precision agriculture platforms, The asymmetry between farmer data generation and platform value extraction, Cooperative versus corporate models for agricultural data governance, Technology dependency risks and platform obsolescence in rural contexts, Practical negotiation strategies for rural technology adoption.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: Quesnel Community Foundation

Sources discussed:

  • Hackaday: [Hackaday] The Smallest Dialup ISP is a Raspberry Pi and a P...
  • XDA Developers: [XDA Developers] A single mini PC replaced my Raspberry Pi c...

  • NYT Top Stories: [NYT Top Stories] Women Need Treatment for Hot Flashes. Why ...
  • NYT Business: [NYT Business] Orion Samuelson, the Elvis Presley of Agricul...

Additional sources provided:

  • Quartz: [Quartz] Is EQT Well-Positioned to Gain on Mounting Clean En...

  • InsideEVs: [InsideEVs] Nissan's Turnaround Hinges On This Unique Hybrid...
  • The Marginalian: [The Marginalian] The Pattern Inside the Pattern: Fractals, ...
  • XDA Developers: [XDA Developers] Wine 11.6 just made modding Windows games o...
  • Hackaday: [Hackaday] Tracking Parts Box Usage With Stickers
  • Hackaday: [Hackaday] Exposing a Radiation-Hardened 2.4 GHz Wi-Fi Recei...
  • Neowin: [Neowin] Incredible AI mod helps unsupported Intel CPU, chip...
  • Hackaday: [Hackaday] Scrap Vintage Camera Goes Digital with Scanner Pa...
  • ScienceAlert: [ScienceAlert] The Prettiest Sea Slugs Use Crystals in Their...
  • TechCrunch: [TechCrunch] Peter Thiel’s big bet on solar-powered cow coll...

Credits

item.itunes:summary:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Resilient Rural Futures.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Hackaday] The Smallest Dialup ISP is a Raspberry Pi and a Prison Phone and [XDA Developers] A single mini PC replaced my Raspberry Pi cluster and nearly cut my power use in half, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: When precision agriculture technology works effectively, how should rural farmers engage with the data ownership and platform dependency structures that come with it? Topics include: Data ownership and control in precision agriculture platforms, The asymmetry between farmer data generation and platform value extraction, Cooperative versus corporate models for agricultural data governance, Technology dependency risks and platform obsolescence in rural contexts, Practical negotiation strategies for rural technology adoption.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: Quesnel Community Foundation

Sources discussed:

  • Hackaday: [Hackaday] The Smallest Dialup ISP is a Raspberry Pi and a P...
  • XDA Developers: [XDA Developers] A single mini PC replaced my Raspberry Pi c...
  • NYT Top Stories: [NYT Top Stories] For Many Patients Leaving the I.C.U., the ...
  • NYT Top Stories: [NYT Top Stories] Women Need Treatment for Hot Flashes. Why ...
  • NYT Business: [NYT Business] Orion Samuelson, the Elvis Presley of Agricul...

Additional sources provided:

  • Quartz: [Quartz] Is EQT Well-Positioned to Gain on Mounting Clean En...
  • How-To Geek: [How-To Geek] Want a private homelab? Put these 12 apps at t...
  • How-To Geek: [How-To Geek] My single homelab server became a nightmare, s...
  • ZDNet: [ZDNet] Adjusting my router's antenna fixed my Wi-Fi problem...
  • InsideEVs: [InsideEVs] Nissan's Turnaround Hinges On This Unique Hybrid...
  • The Marginalian: [The Marginalian] The Pattern Inside the Pattern: Fractals, ...
  • XDA Developers: [XDA Developers] Wine 11.6 just made modding Windows games o...
  • Hackaday: [Hackaday] Tracking Parts Box Usage With Stickers
  • Hackaday: [Hackaday] Exposing a Radiation-Hardened 2.4 GHz Wi-Fi Recei...
  • Neowin: [Neowin] Incredible AI mod helps unsupported Intel CPU, chip...
  • Hackaday: [Hackaday] Scrap Vintage Camera Goes Digital with Scanner Pa...
  • ScienceAlert: [ScienceAlert] The Prettiest Sea Slugs Use Crystals in Their...
  • TechCrunch: [TechCrunch] Peter Thiel’s big bet on solar-powered cow coll...

Credits
Theme Song: Cariboo Signals by Suno
Content Curation & Script: Claude (Anthropic)
Script Review Model: claude-opus-4-6
TTS Voices: OpenAI TTS API
Cover Art: ChatGPT (OpenAI)
Podcast Coordination: Erich Zirnhelt
© 2026 Erich Zirnhelt. Licensed under CC BY-NC 4.0.

item.title: Cariboo Voices And Local News
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Cariboo Voices & Local News.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Williams Lake Tribune] Prince George RCMP investigate after object strikes paramedic inside ambulance and [Hackaday] Reverse-Engineering a Handheld Car Tire Pressure Gauge, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: How can local accountability infrastructure—particularly journalism—be sustainably funded and structured to serve rural communities like the Cariboo? Topics include: Elections BC enforcement and formal political accountability mechanisms, Local journalism sustainability and the business model crisis in rural media, First Nations governance and Indigenous data sovereignty as parallel accountability structures, Civic tech tools and their sustainability challenges in rural contexts, Community foundation endowment models as durable funding alternatives.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

COMMUNITY SPOTLIGHT: Quesnel Community Foundation

Sources discussed:

  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Prince George RCMP investigate after...
  • Hackaday: [Hackaday] Reverse-Engineering a Handheld Car Tire Pressure ...
  • Hackaday: [Hackaday] Simulating a glowing fireplace with an RP2040
  • ScienceAlert: [ScienceAlert] This New Sensor Could Sniff Out Pneumonia on ...
  • TechRadar: [TechRadar] 'Copilot is for entertainment purposes only': Ev...
  • Quesnel Cariboo Observer: [Quesnel Cariboo Observer] Prince George-North Cariboo MLA f...

Additional sources provided:

  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Repeat offender sentenced to 5 years...
  • Nautilus: [Nautilus] Cosmic Mysteries Swirl Around “Forbidden Planet”
  • TechRadar: [TechRadar] Forget the tower: Dell’s palm-sized desktop draw...
  • Gizmodo: [Gizmodo] Utah Is Giving Dr. AI the Power to Renew Drug Pres...
  • InsideEVs: [InsideEVs] EV Depreciation Is Still Brutal. But Hybrids Sho...
  • Android Authority: [Android Authority] Microsoft put the same disclaimer on Cop...
  • Nautilus: [Nautilus] Your Biological Clock Can be Measured With a Hair...
  • How-To Geek: [How-To Geek] My flat Ethernet cable was silently cutting my...
  • Business Insider: [Business Insider] Automakers are teaming up, speeding up, a...
  • ScienceAlert: [ScienceAlert] Eerie Cosmic-Ray 'Cavity' Found Lurking in Ea...
  • Gizmodo: [Gizmodo] Rubin Observatory Spots Over 11,000 Asteroids in R...
  • InsideEVs: [InsideEVs] BYD Has Already Built 5,000 Of Its New Megawatt ...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] UPDATE: Highway 1 near Chase re-open...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Canada’s 7th Most Wanted Arrested in...

Credits
Theme Song: Cariboo Signals by Suno
Content Curation & Script: Claude (Anthropic)
Script Review Model: claude-sonnet-4-6
TTS Voices: OpenAI TTS API
Cover Art: ChatGPT (OpenAI)
Podcast Coordination: Erich Zirnhelt

item.title: Wild Spaces And Outdoor Life
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Wild Spaces & Outdoor Life.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [The Tyee] A Deadly Fungal Invader Is Threatening BC’s Bats and [BC Gov News] Opening Clean Industry Fund 2026 intake, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: Who controls ecological knowledge and data in Cariboo wilderness, and does technology democratization actually distribute power or concentrate it further? Topics include: Data sovereignty and Indigenous data governance frameworks (OCAP principles, UNDRIP implementation), Co-management models for monitoring infrastructure versus province-run systems, Citizen science limitations and strengths in low-population-density rural regions, Backcountry safety infrastructure (satellite communicators, SAR funding models), Maintenance and sustainability of remote monitoring equipment in harsh climates.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

Sources discussed:

  • The Tyee: [The Tyee] A Deadly Fungal Invader Is Threatening BC’s Bats
  • BC Gov News: [BC Gov News] Opening Clean Industry Fund 2026 intake
  • Android Authority: [Android Authority] The DJI Power 1000 V2 is a no-nonsense p...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Spring Classic Indoor Rodeo returns ...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Williams Lake Punjabi Society hostin...
  • Quesnel Cariboo Observer: [Quesnel Cariboo Observer] 20-year-old man missing since Apr...
  • Quesnel Cariboo Observer: [Quesnel Cariboo Observer] Prince George RCMP has arrested a...
  • Civil Eats: [Civil Eats] USDA Pauses Rural Energy Grants Amid Anti-Renew...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Injured beaver from Williams Lake tr...

Additional sources provided:

  • 100 Mile Free Press: [100 Mile Free Press] BUXTON: Bird watching is not just for ...
  • Gizmodo: [Gizmodo] China Is Trying to Grow New Forests in This Notori...
  • The Tyee: [The Tyee] BC Government May Use AI to Redact Personal Infor...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Hero’s welcome home to ‘village’ for...
  • Business Insider: [Business Insider] Uber drivers: Your boss knows you're usin...
  • Hackaday: [Hackaday] Pan and Tilt The Weatherproof Way, With Bowden Ca...
  • TechRadar: [TechRadar] HP unveils its most powerful PC ever with up to ...
  • Quartz: [Quartz] Hims & Hers Expands Personalized Digital Healthcare...
  • Quartz: [Quartz] UUUU's Terbium Oxide Breakthrough: Turning Point fo...
  • Quartz: [Quartz] MP's Loss Streak Continued in 2025: Can It Stage a ...
  • Quartz: [Quartz] SolarEdge Technologies Expands US Footprint to Powe...
  • Gizmodo: [Gizmodo] Cursor’s New Tool Lets Users Delegate to a Team of...
  • ScienceAlert: [ScienceAlert] Chemical Signature Hidden in Lunar Rocks Hint...

Credits
Theme Song: Cariboo Signals by Suno
Content Curation & Script: Claude (Anthropic)
Script Review Model: claude-opus-4-6

Cover Art: ChatGPT (OpenAI)

item.title: Indigenous Lands And Innovation
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Indigenous Lands & Innovation.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [Engadget] What’s going on with Donut Lab? and [Williams Lake Tribune] British Columbians declare 15,600 assault-style firearms in buyback program, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: What does it mean for technology and institutional processes to genuinely serve Indigenous self-determination rather than adding complexity or enabling external control? Topics include: DRIPA implementation failures and capacity constraints in consultation processes, Indigenous language revitalization technology and maintenance sustainability, Data sovereignty and environmental monitoring with regulatory authority, Community-owned versus externally-deployed infrastructure models, Maintenance funding as the critical but underfunded layer of technology sustainability.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

Sources discussed:

  • Engadget: [Engadget] What’s going on with Donut Lab?
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] British Columbians declare 15,600 as...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] B.C. man calling on feds to declare ...
  • Hackaday: [Hackaday] Every 3D Printable Film Camera, In One Place
  • ScienceAlert: [ScienceAlert] Your Vision Can Predict Dementia 12 Years Bef...
  • Gizmodo: [Gizmodo] Kia’s EV3 Is Headed to the US This Year With Up to...
  • BC Gov News: [BC Gov News] Updating zero-emission vehicle targets, expand...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Interior Health issues drug alert in...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] 108 Heritage Site in running for $50...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Fire extinguished at 100 Mile House ...
  • Ricochet: [Ricochet] ‘How many more have to die?’: Northern Ontario fi...

Additional sources provided:

  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Court fines visitors for 2 dog incid...
  • Toms Guide: [Toms Guide] Your soil might still be compacted from winter ...
  • NYT Top Stories: [NYT Top Stories] China’s Aiming for the Moon, and NASA Is L...
  • Quartz: [Quartz] Ormat Technologies Expands Growth via Geothermal an...
  • XDA Developers: [XDA Developers] I built a clipboard server on the Arduino U...
  • CNET: [CNET] Robots Could Help Humans Find Resources on the Moon a...
  • TechRadar: [TechRadar] 'Defying logic': This quantum battery actually c...
  • Quesnel Cariboo Observer: [Quesnel Cariboo Observer] Prince George RCMP arrest man aft...
  • Nautilus: [Nautilus] How Sleep Cleans the Brain
  • XDA Developers: [XDA Developers] Microsoft finally admitted Windows 11 was b...
  • XDA Developers: [XDA Developers] 4 one-click BIOS changes that unlock the PC...
  • Kottke.org: [Kottke.org] From Heatmap News & MIT, the Electricity Price...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] First Nations letter calls B.C. gove...
  • Kottke.org: [Kottke.org] On Stewart Brand’s Maintenance: Of Everyt...

Credits
Theme Song: Cariboo Signals by Suno

Script Review Model: claude-sonnet-4-6

item.title: Community Tech And Governance
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Community Tech & Governance.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [BC Gov News] Building volunteer fire department capacity and [Android Authority] Android Auto just made EV trip planning a lot smarter for these car brands, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: How do information systems and governance structures in rural communities determine who has access to decision-making power, and whose interests do transparency measures actually serve? Topics include: Cougar alert systems and emergency communication infrastructure in rural BC, Canada Post mail delivery consolidation and loss of bundled community services, Aboriginal Title Transparency Act and strategic disclosure in Indigenous governance, Epistemic infrastructure and who controls information about community needs, Heiltsuk and other Indigenous-led information sovereignty models.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

Sources discussed:

  • BC Gov News: [BC Gov News] Building volunteer fire department capacity
  • Android Authority: [Android Authority] Android Auto just made EV trip planning ...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] TRAILBLAZER CHRONICLES: Bathrooms, b...
  • TechRadar: [TechRadar] Iran’s internet shutdown proves we need to go be...
  • 100 Mile Free Press: [100 Mile Free Press] Vehicle stolen in Chilliwack recovered...
  • Nautilus: [Nautilus] Who Gets to Do Science?
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] MLA McInnis introduces Aboriginal Ti...
  • 100 Mile Free Press: [100 Mile Free Press] Potential cougar sighted in Centennial...

Additional sources provided:

  • Boing Boing: [Boing Boing] Audio tapes challenge Milgram obedience findin...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Deadline looms for property owner to...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] B.C. moving to alter environment ass...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Prescribed burns set for Nemiah Vall...
  • Toms Guide: [Toms Guide] I put Claude vs. Deepseek through 7 real-world ...
  • Hackaday: [Hackaday] Running a Game on a PC with no System RAM
  • Business Insider: [Business Insider] Inside the OpenAI project where freelance...
  • Business Insider: [Business Insider] Anthropic accidentally exposed part of Cl...
  • Neowin: [Neowin] Mercor says it is "one of thousands of companies" h...
  • ZDNet: [ZDNet] How AI has suddenly become much more useful to open-...
  • WIRED: [WIRED] Robotaxi Outage in China Leaves Passengers Stranded ...
  • Quartz: [Quartz] Robots, cameras, and lots of data about garbage: In...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Canada Post to convert remaining doo...

Credits
Theme Song: Cariboo Signals by Suno
Content Curation & Script: Claude (Anthropic)
Script Review Model: claude-sonnet-4-6
TTS Voices: OpenAI TTS API
Cover Art: ChatGPT (OpenAI)
Podcast Coordination: Erich Zirnhelt
© 2026 Erich Zirnhelt. Licensed under CC BY-NC 4.0.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [BC Gov News] Building volunteer fire department capacity and [Android Authority] Android Auto just made EV trip planning a lot smarter for these car brands, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: How do information systems and governance structures in rural communities determine who has access to decision-making power, and whose interests do transparency measures actually serve? item.title: Working Lands And Industry
item.description:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Working Lands & Industry.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [The Tyee] Half of BC’s Community Paramedic Positions Are Unfilled and [Williams Lake Tribune] New Democrats pass resolution to protect northwest B.C. coastal tanker ban, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: How can the Cariboo build a working landscape that is economically viable, ecologically sustainable, and socially equitable when these three goals are often in tension? Topics include: BC Timber Sales policy design and competitive market dynamics in forestry, Soil health science, microbial inoculants, and measurement challenges for ranching, Carbon markets, ecosystem service payments, and aggregation models for smallholder operations, Indigenous co-governance, traditional ecological knowledge, and tenure restructuring, Infrastructure gaps: connectivity, processing facilities, long-term capital, and organizational capacity.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

Sources discussed:

item.itunes:summary:
Riley and Casey explore technology and society in rural communities. Today's focus: Working Lands & Industry.

NEWS ROUNDUP: We break down [The Tyee] Half of BC’s Community Paramedic Positions Are Unfilled and [Williams Lake Tribune] New Democrats pass resolution to protect northwest B.C. coastal tanker ban, plus 1 more stories, and explore what these developments mean for communities like ours.

RURAL CONNECTIONS: How can the Cariboo build a working landscape that is economically viable, ecologically sustainable, and socially equitable when these three goals are often in tension? Topics include: BC Timber Sales policy design and competitive market dynamics in forestry, Soil health science, microbial inoculants, and measurement challenges for ranching, Carbon markets, ecosystem service payments, and aggregation models for smallholder operations, Indigenous co-governance, traditional ecological knowledge, and tenure restructuring, Infrastructure gaps: connectivity, processing facilities, long-term capital, and organizational capacity.

Hosts: Riley (Tech optimist with an engineering background) and Casey (Community-first skeptic of tech promises).

Sources discussed:

  • The Tyee: [The Tyee] Half of BC’s Community Paramedic Positions Are Un...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] New Democrats pass resolution to pro...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Williams Lake celebrates young piani...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Volunteers help local children facin...
  • Quesnel Cariboo Observer: [Quesnel Cariboo Observer] Lhtako Dene Nation launches long-...
  • Williams Lake Tribune: [Williams Lake Tribune] Doctors say regulatory decisions cou...
  • BC Gov News: [BC Gov News] BC Timber Sales to increase fibre access, stre...
